 {"id":"wa-3mus","title":"FTUI-08.3.a Compatibility runbook template per terminal/mux environment","description":"## Background
Compatibility certification must be repeatable across terminal/mux environments with uniform evidence quality.

## Deliverables
- runbook template defining environment metadata, test scope, expected outcomes, and failure policy
- required evidence checklist (logs, snapshots, command outputs, known limitations)
- comparison rubric for pass/conditional-pass/fail decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- template can be reused for each target environment without interpretation drift
- evidence requirements explicitly include unit/E2E references and logging expectations
- resulting reports are concise, actionable, and suitable for release gating.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:42.357592844Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:16:09.620545016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:16:09.620416958Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3mus","depends_on_id":"wa-e69a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:42.374372734Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3mus","depends_on_id":"wa-308u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:43.785878589Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3p06","title":"Auth tokens + capability scopes for IPC","description":"## What
Implement token-based authentication and capability scopes for local IPC.

## Why
Local IPC should not be a privilege escalation path.

## How
- Generate short-lived tokens with scope (read-only vs mutating)
- Validate tokens on every request
- Reuse PolicyEngine for mutating actions

## Success Criteria
- Invalid/expired tokens rejected with stable errors
- Scope enforced for each method","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:33.145217948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:10:08.448808287Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:10:08.448735371Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3p06","depends_on_id":"wa-3iax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3pc9","title":"FTUI-05.2 Migrate Home dashboard view to ftui widgets","description":"## Background\nHome view is primary operator entry point and sets baseline migration pattern.\n\n## Deliverables\n- home metrics/cards/status sections in ftui\n- refresh and small-terminal behavior parity\n- parity checklist for all home widgets\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- home view matches parity contract or records intentional deltas\n- no panics on constrained terminal sizes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:10.45926505Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:23:09.105093548Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:23:09.105029579Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3pc9","depends_on_id":"wa-38vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:10.489106659Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pc9","depends_on_id":"wa-1hbj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:39.656394152Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pc9","depends_on_id":"wa-sx5v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:49.366772346Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
-{"id":"wa-3pr0","title":"Cross-Pane Correlation Engine","description":"# Cross-Pane Correlation Engine

## Goal
Detect simultaneous or causally-related events across multiple panes using statistical correlation analysis (chi-squared co-occurrence testing).

## How it works
When events occur across multiple panes within a configurable time window:
1. Build a co-occurrence matrix of (pane_i, event_type) pairs
2. Apply chi-squared test for statistical significance
3. Report correlated events with p-values

## Use cases
- Rate limit hits across multiple agents → shared API key exhaustion
- Error cascades: one agent failure triggers others
- Build coordination: multiple agents hitting same compilation
- Pattern: "whenever pane A errors, pane B errors within 30s"

## Integration
- Receives events from pattern detection engine (patterns.rs)
- Publishes correlation events to event bus
- Feeds into wa-283h4.1 (Causal DAG) which adds directionality

## Relationship to other beads
- **wa-283h4.1 (Causal DAG)**: This bead detects CO-OCCURRENCE (events happen together). The causal DAG detects CAUSATION (event A causes event B, with direction and lag). This is the prerequisite.
- **wa-283h4.11 (LSH error clustering)**: This detects SIMILAR errors across panes. Cross-pane correlation detects SIMULTANEOUS events (which may be different events).
- **wa-1l2o (Circuit breaker)**: Uses correlation output to detect cascades.

## Tests
- Synthetic event streams with known correlations → verify detection
- Chi-squared test correctly identifies significant vs spurious correlations
- **Criterion benchmarks**: correlation check for 50 panes × 10 event types (<5ms)
- **proptest**: random event streams never produce false positives above expected rate
- Integration: inject correlated events, verify correlation event published

## Acceptance criteria
- Co-occurrence matrix maintained incrementally
- Chi-squared significance test with configurable p-value threshold
- Correlation events published to event bus
- <5ms per correlation check for 50 panes
- False positive rate below 5% at p=0.01 threshold","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:43:41.964036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:11:13.07452-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:23.312191Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:39.39782Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.5","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:16:55.078087Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
+{"id":"wa-3pr0","title":"Cross-Pane Correlation Engine","description":"# Cross-Pane Correlation Engine

## Goal
Detect simultaneous or causally-related events across multiple panes using statistical correlation analysis (chi-squared co-occurrence testing).

## How it works
When events occur across multiple panes within a configurable time window:
1. Build a co-occurrence matrix of (pane_i, event_type) pairs
2. Apply chi-squared test for statistical significance
3. Report correlated events with p-values

## Use cases
- Rate limit hits across multiple agents → shared API key exhaustion
- Error cascades: one agent failure triggers others
- Build coordination: multiple agents hitting same compilation
- Pattern: "whenever pane A errors, pane B errors within 30s"

## Integration
- Receives events from pattern detection engine (patterns.rs)
- Publishes correlation events to event bus
- Feeds into wa-283h4.1 (Causal DAG) which adds directionality

## Relationship to other beads
- **wa-283h4.1 (Causal DAG)**: This bead detects CO-OCCURRENCE (events happen together). The causal DAG detects CAUSATION (event A causes event B, with direction and lag). This is the prerequisite.
- **wa-283h4.11 (LSH error clustering)**: This detects SIMILAR errors across panes. Cross-pane correlation detects SIMULTANEOUS events (which may be different events).
- **wa-1l2o (Circuit breaker)**: Uses correlation output to detect cascades.

## Tests
- Synthetic event streams with known correlations → verify detection
- Chi-squared test correctly identifies significant vs spurious correlations
- **Criterion benchmarks**: correlation check for 50 panes × 10 event types (<5ms)
- **proptest**: random event streams never produce false positives above expected rate
- Integration: inject correlated events, verify correlation event published

## Acceptance criteria
- Co-occurrence matrix maintained incrementally
- Chi-squared significance test with configurable p-value threshold
- Correlation events published to event bus
- <5ms per correlation check for 50 panes
- False positive rate below 5% at p=0.01 threshold","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:43:41.964036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:30:37.059551-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:30:37.059551-05:00","close_reason":"Cross-pane correlation engine implemented with chi-squared co-occurrence testing, 23 tests passing","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:23.312191Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:39.39782Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-3pr0","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.5","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:16:55.078087Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3q7q","title":"SQLite tables for snapshot storage and scrollback dedup","description":"## Goal
Extend wa's existing SQLite database (schema v20+) to store mux snapshots persistently, with efficient querying and retention management.

## Background
wa already has a mature SQLite storage layer with WAL mode, schema versioning, 13+ tables including panes, output_segments, events, agent_sessions, etc. The snapshot storage should follow the same patterns: epoch-ms timestamps, proper indexing, and retention policies.

## Design
1. New table `mux_snapshots`:
   - id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
   - snapshot_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (UUID)
   - created_at INTEGER NOT NULL (epoch ms)
   - trigger TEXT NOT NULL ('periodic', 'manual', 'pre_restart', 'pre_upgrade')
   - mux_server_pid INTEGER
   - wezterm_version TEXT
   - wa_version TEXT
   - window_count INTEGER
   - tab_count INTEGER
   - pane_count INTEGER
   - snapshot_json TEXT NOT NULL (full MuxSnapshot as JSON)
   - snapshot_size_bytes INTEGER
   - checksum TEXT (SHA-256 of snapshot_json for integrity)

2. New table `snapshot_scrollbacks`:
   - id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
   - snapshot_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES mux_snapshots(snapshot_id)
   - pane_id INTEGER NOT NULL
   - content_hash TEXT NOT NULL (for dedup across snapshots)
   - content TEXT NOT NULL (escaped terminal output)
   - line_count INTEGER
   - byte_count INTEGER
   - UNIQUE(snapshot_id, pane_id)

3. Content-addressed scrollback dedup:
   - If two consecutive snapshots have the same scrollback hash for a pane, only store it once
   - Reference by content_hash to avoid duplicating multi-MB scrollback buffers

4. Retention policy:
   - Keep last N snapshots (configurable, default 10)
   - Keep all snapshots from last 24 hours
   - Keep at least 1 snapshot per day for last 7 days
   - Configurable via wa.toml [snapshots] section

5. Schema migration:
   - Increment schema version to 21 (or next available)
   - Add migration in storage.rs following existing patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tables created on schema upgrade
- Insert and query snapshots with proper indexing
- Scrollback dedup verified (same content only stored once)
- Retention cleanup works correctly
- Migration from v20 to v21 is clean

## Benchmark & Property Testing Requirements

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add `benches/snapshot_sqlite.rs` using criterion:
- **insert_latency**: Benchmark inserting a full MuxSnapshot (50 panes, 5000 lines each) into the mux_snapshots and snapshot_scrollbacks tables. Measure wall-clock time and I/O bytes. Target: <500ms for a typical 50-pane snapshot.
- **query_latest_snapshot**: Benchmark querying the most recent snapshot by created_at. Target: <5ms with proper indexing.
- **query_by_trigger**: Benchmark querying snapshots filtered by trigger type. Target: <10ms.
- **dedup_savings**: Benchmark insert time when content_hash matches an existing row (dedup path) vs fresh insert. Measure storage savings for 10 consecutive snapshots where 80% of panes have unchanged scrollback.
- **retention_cleanup**: Benchmark the retention cleanup query (DELETE old snapshots) with 100, 500, 1000 snapshots in the table.

### Proptest
Add `tests/proptest_snapshot_sqlite.rs`:
- **schema_migration_correctness**: For any arbitrary initial database state (proptest generates random existing rows in pre-migration tables), assert that migration to v21 preserves all existing data and creates new tables without errors. Run the migration, then verify all pre-existing rows are intact and new tables exist with correct schema.
- **retention_policy_invariants**: For any sequence of snapshot inserts with arbitrary timestamps and trigger types (proptest generates), assert that after cleanup: (a) at most N snapshots remain, (b) all snapshots from the last 24 hours are retained, (c) at least 1 snapshot per day for the last 7 days is retained.
- **dedup_correctness**: For any sequence of (snapshot_id, pane_id, content) triples where some share the same content, assert that only one copy of each unique content exists in storage, and all references resolve correctly.

## Cross-References
- **wa-283h4.2** (WAL snapshots): The WAL (Write-Ahead Log) snapshot system provides a complementary persistence mechanism. While wa-3q7q stores discrete point-in-time snapshots, WAL snapshots capture a continuous log of state changes. The two systems should share the same SQLite database and coordinate schema versions. WAL snapshots can reference wa-3q7q's snapshot_id as a "checkpoint" -- the WAL only needs to store deltas since the last full snapshot, dramatically reducing WAL size.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:17:31.871479Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.21032-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:46:44.982738-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: storage.rs schema v21 with mux_sessions, session_checkpoints, mux_pane_state tables. Includes cascade deletes, constraint validation, BLAKE3 dedup, multi-host support.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3q7q","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:34:45.327547Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-3qq6","title":"CLI: config profile list/create/apply","description":"## What
Implement profile management commands.

## Why
Operators need a simple CLI workflow for switching configurations.

## How
- `wa config profile list`
- `wa config profile create <name> --from current`
- `wa config profile apply <name>`
- Validate profiles before apply

## Success Criteria
- CLI commands operate without modifying unrelated config keys
- Errors are actionable and include diffs on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:35.837900509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T09:07:27.227918473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T09:07:27.227849755Z"}
